name: British Columbia (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean on which the province lies
the wavy stripes, also the Union Jack
the setting sun and the imperial crown

description: Banner of arms: the upper half displays a Union Jack defaced with a gold antique crown in the centre; the lower half shows a half-sun setting over four wavy blue stripes alternating with three white stripes, representing the Pacific Ocean.
history: Adopted on 27 June 1960, the design is a banner of the provincial coat of arms granted by King Edward VII in 1906 (with the crown added later). The Union Jack and crown reflect British heritage; the sun and waves represent the province's Pacific location and its status as Canada's westernmost province.

features.role: Union Jack across the upper half
features.symbolism: British Columbia's status as a former British colony and its heritage within the United Kingdom

features.role: antique gold crown centred over the Union Jack
features.symbolism: the British sovereign and the province's monarchical heritage

features.role: half-sun setting over wavy blue and white stripes
features.symbolism: the setting sun symbolises British Columbia's location at the western edge of the country, where the sun sets on Canada
